priority: Your work is document-primary. The monitor is the most important purchase.
2D drafting is single-thread CPU bound but not demanding. Display quality matters more than compute.
BIM modeling is single-thread CPU bound. Clock speed governs how Revit, Rhino, and SketchUp feel. The GPU is nearly invisible.

Interactive viz is GPU bound. Enscape, Lumion, D5, TwinMotion scale directly with GPU power and VRAM. GPU prices are inflated in early 2026 due to a GDDR7 memory shortage -- the 5070 Ti is the best value right now.
You need both: fast single-core for modeling and a capable GPU for viz. The 5070 Ti at ~$1,000 is the honest sweet spot in early 2026.

V-Ray and Corona are multi-thread CPU bound with GPU hybrid modes. The Ryzen 9 9950X leads. Note: the 5090 is severely marked up in early 2026. The 5080 is the better value for GPU hybrid rendering right now.
Document-primary. The display matters more than the Mac.
2D drafting on Mac is well-served by the M4 Pro.
ArchiCAD, Vectorworks, SketchUp, Rhino v8 all run on Mac. Performance scales with GPU cores and unified memory.
Enscape works on Mac. TwinMotion runs via Rosetta. Lumion and D5 are Windows-only.
Unified memory serves as both RAM and VRAM.
V-Ray supports Metal GPU since V-Ray 7. A mid-range Windows PC with an RTX 4070 still outrenders a maxed Mac Studio. Mac wins on ecosystem, display, and silence.
